initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<example_1>
<example_grid_1>

7 7 7
0 7 7

7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 7 7 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
</diagram>
</example_grid_1>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Count rows, columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% cell predicate
cell(R,C) :- R < N, C < M.

% start predicate (non-zero cells)
start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% For each cell, output chosen
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Define covered (i.e., start column <= column)
cover(R,C) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0.

% parity even
even(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*E.

% parity odd
odd(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*E + 1.

% allowed colors
allowed(R,C,X) :- even(R,C,V), X = V.
allowed(R,C,X) :- odd(R,C,V), X = 5.
allowed(R,C,X) :- not cover(R,C), X = 0.

% Forbid non-allowed colors
:- cell(R,C), output(R,C,X), not allowed(R,C,X).

% Count rows and cols
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% cell for each output position
cell(R,C) :- R < N, C < M.

% start (non-zero input cells)
start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% cover: start column <= current column
cover(R,C) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0.

% parity even
even(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, Diff # = 2*F, Diff = C - C0.

% parity odd
odd(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, Diff # = 2*F + 1, Diff = C - C0.

% allowed colors
allowed(R,C,Color) :- even(R,C,V), Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- odd(R,C,V), Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not cover(R,C), Color = 0.

% Output with exactly one color per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Forbid non-allowed colors
:- cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

% Count rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% cell for each output position
cell(R,C) :- R < N, C < M.

% start cells (non-zero values)
start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% cover (there is a start in this row with column <= C)
cover(R,C) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0.

% parity even
even(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F.

% parity odd
odd(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F + 1.

% allowed colors for each output cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- even(R,C,V), Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- odd(R,C,V), Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not cover(R,C), Color = 0.

% exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any color that is not allowed
:- cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

full_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Row‑wise rule inferred from the three training examples
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Count how many non‑zero cells a row contains
row_nz_cnt(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }.

% Row categories
row_has_one(R) :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 1.
row_zero(R)   :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 0.
row_multi(R)  :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz > 1.

% The unique non‑zero cell in a “one‑non‑zero” row
pivot(R,P,V) :- row_has_one(R), input(R,P,V), V != 0.

% Every position that occurs in the input (including zeros)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Allowed colours for each cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Row with exactly one non‑zero cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C = P,      Color = V.   % pivot column
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C <  P,     Color = 0.   % left of pivot
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C >  P, (C - P) = 2*F,     Color = V. % right, even offset
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C >  P, (C - P) = 2*F + 1, Color = 5. % right, odd offset

% Rows without any non‑zero cell become all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_zero(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% Rows with more than one non‑zero cell become all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% No colour may be chosen outside the allowed set
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% count distinct rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% a cell in the output grid must receive a colour
cell(R,C) :- R < N, C < M.

% start cells are the non‑zero cells of the input
start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% there is a start in this row whose column is not to the right of C
cover(R,C) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0.

% parity even: (C‑C0) is a multiple of 2
even(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F.

% parity odd: (C‑C0) is one more than a multiple of 2
odd(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F + 1.

% allowed colour for a cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- even(R,C,V), Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- odd(R,C,V),   Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not cover(R,C), Color = 0.

% exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed
:- cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

% Determine number of rows and columns
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% cell predicate
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non-zero cells per row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot for rows with exactly one non-zero cell
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% allowed colors for cells
% rows without any non-zero cell: all cells are 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% allowed for pivot column
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% for cells to the left of pivot
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% for cells to the right of pivot with even offset: color = V
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, C - P = 2*F, cell(R,C).

% for cells to the right of pivot with odd offset: color = 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1, cell(R,C).

% each cell must have a color that is allowed
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid colors that are not allowed
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
```

% count rows and columns
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% valid cell coordinates
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non-zero cells per row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot cell for rows that have exactly one non-zero

% allowed colors for each cell

allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% pivot column cell – output the original colour
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% cells left of the pivot – colour 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot – even distance -> original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, C - P = 2*F, cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot – odd distance -> colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1, cell(R,C).

% exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>
```

Define:

% Count rows and columns
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% cell predicate
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non-zero per row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot for rows with exactly one non-zero (leftmost)
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% allowed colors
% rows without any non-zero: all zeros
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% pivot column: original value
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% left of pivot: zeros
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% right of pivot, even offset -> original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), even_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% right of pivot, odd offset -> colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), odd_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% define parity helpers
even_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F.
odd_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1.

% exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid non‑allowed colours
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

% Count rows and columns
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% cell predicate
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non-zero per row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot for rows with exactly one non-zero (leftmost)
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% allowed colors
% rows without any non-zero: all zeros
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% pivot column: original value
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% left of pivot: zeros
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% right of pivot, even offset -> original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), even_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% right of pivot, odd offset -> colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), odd_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% define parity helpers
even_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F.
odd_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1.

% exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

refinements.response:
<asp>
% count the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% cell (row, col) is inside the input grid
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non‑zero cells in each row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot: the (only) non‑zero cell of a row with exactly one such cell
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% ----- allowed colours for every cell -----
% rows without any non‑zero cell: all cells must be 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% the pivot cell keeps the original colour
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% cells left of the pivot are forced to 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with even offset keep the original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), even_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with odd offset become colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), odd_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% parity helpers (used by the two rules above)
even_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F.
odd_offset(R,C,P,F)  :- C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1.

% exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed for the cell
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% count distinct rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% a cell in the output grid must receive a colour
cell(R,C) :- R < N, C < M.

% start cells are the non‑zero cells of the input
start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% there is a start in this row whose column is not to the right of C
cover(R,C) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0.

% parity even: (C‑C0) is a multiple of 2
even(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F.

% parity odd: (C‑C0) is one more than a multiple of 2
odd(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F + 1.

% allowed colour for a cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- even(R,C,V), Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- odd(R,C,V),   Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not cover(R,C), Color = 0.

% exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed
:- cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

<attempt_2>
<asp>
% count the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% cell (row, col) is inside the input grid
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non‑zero cells in each row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot: the (only) non‑zero cell of a row with exactly one such cell
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% ----- allowed colours for every cell -----
% rows without any non‑zero cell: all cells must be 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% the pivot cell keeps the original colour
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% cells left of the pivot are forced to 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with even offset keep the original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), even_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with odd offset become colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), odd_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% parity helpers (used by the two rules above)
even_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F.
odd_offset(R,C,P,F)  :- C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1.

% exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed for the cell
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

<asp>
% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Row has exactly one non‑zero cell
row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

% Row has no non‑zero cells
row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non‑zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates (all rows and columns that appear in the input)
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Allowed colours for each cell
% pivot cells
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
% cells left of the pivot
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
% cells to the right with odd offset -> colour 5
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
% cells to the right with even offset -> keep original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 0, Color = V.

% rows without any non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), Color = 0.

% rows with more than one non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any colour that is not permitted
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Row has exactly one non-zero
row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

% Row has no non-zero cells
row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non-zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 0, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any colour that is not allowed
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

<asp>
% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Row has exactly one non‑zero cell
row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

% Row has no non‑zero cells
row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non‑zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates (all rows and columns that appear in the input)
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 0, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any colour that is not permitted
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

% Non-zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Row has exactly one non‑zero cell
row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

% Row has no non‑zero cell
row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non‑zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates (all rows and columns that appear in the input)
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 0, Color = V.

% rows without any non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), Color = 0.

% rows with more than one non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any colour that is not permitted
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non‑zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates (all rows and columns that appear in the input)
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow colours that are not allowed

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% count the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% cell (row, col) is inside the input grid
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non‑zero cells in each row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot: the (only) non‑zero cell of a row with exactly one such cell
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% ----- allowed colours for every cell -----
% rows without any non‑zero cell: all cells must be 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% the pivot cell keeps the original colour
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% cells left of the pivot are forced to 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with even offset keep the original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), even_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with odd offset become colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), odd_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% parity helpers (used by the two rules above)
even_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F.
odd_offset(R,C,P,F)  :- C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1.

% exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed for the cell
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

<asp>
% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Row has exactly one non‑zero cell
row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

% Row has no non‑zero cells
row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non‑zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates (all rows and columns that appear in the input)
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 0, Color = V.

% rows without any non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), Color = 0.

% rows with more than one non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any colour that is not permitted
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

<asp>
% A cell is any position that occurs in the input (including zero)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% A non‑zero cell in a row (a “source”)
source(R,Col,V) :- input(R,Col,V), V != 0.

% Does the row contain a source?
row_has_source(R) :- source(R,Col,V).

% Allowed colour for each cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,Col,V), C < Col,        Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,Col,V), C = Col,        Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,Col,V), C > Col, (C - Col) = 2*F,  Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,Col,V), C > Col, (C - Col) = 2*F + 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not row_has_source(R),           Color = 0.

% Exactly one colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow colours that are not allowed
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).
</asp>

% Define rows and columns
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Define source for each non-zero cell
source(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0.

% Define source_exists for row
source_exists(R) :- source(R,P,V).

% Define allowed colors
% same row source
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.

% left side
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.

% right side even distance
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,P,V), C > P, C = P + 2*F, Color = V.

% right side odd distance
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,P,V), C > P, C = P + 2*F + 1, Color = 5.

% rows without source -> all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not source_exists(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% Output exactly one per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any other colors
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

% Define rows and columns present in input
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% 5) Rows without any source are all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not source_exists(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% Exactly one colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% No colour outside the allowed set
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

% Define rows and columns present in the input
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Cell (row, col) of the grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Non‑zero cells (source)
source(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0.

% Row has at least one source – used for the default rule
source_exists(R) :- source(R,P,V).

% Allowed colours for a cell
% 1) The source cell itself keeps its original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- source(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.

% 2) Cells left of the source become 0

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% count distinct rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% a cell in the output grid must receive a colour
cell(R,C) :- R < N, C < M.

% start cells are the non‑zero cells of the input
start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% there is a start in this row whose column is not to the right of C
cover(R,C) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0.

% parity even: (C‑C0) is a multiple of 2
even(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F.

% parity odd: (C‑C0) is one more than a multiple of 2
odd(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F + 1.

% allowed colour for a cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- even(R,C,V), Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- odd(R,C,V),   Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not cover(R,C), Color = 0.

% exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed
:- cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

<asp>
% count the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% cell (row, col) is inside the input grid
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non‑zero cells in each row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot: the (only) non‑zero cell of a row with exactly one such cell
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% ----- allowed colours for every cell -----
% rows without any non‑zero cell: all cells must be 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% the pivot cell keeps the original colour
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% cells left of the pivot are forced to 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with even offset keep the original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), even_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with odd offset become colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), odd_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% parity helpers (used by the two rules above)
even_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F.
odd_offset(R,C,P,F)  :- C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1.

% exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed for the cell
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

<asp>
% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Row has exactly one non‑zero cell
row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

% Row has no non‑zero cells
row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non‑zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates (all rows and columns that appear in the input)
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 0, Color = V.

% rows without any non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), Color = 0.

% rows with more than one non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any colour that is not permitted
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

% Determine rows that have exactly one non-zero cell
row_has_one(R) :- #count {C : input(R, C, V), V != 0} = 1.

% The (unique) non-zero cell in such a row
pivot(R, P, V) :- row_has_one(R), input(R, P, V), V != 0.

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Allowed colors for each cell
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, D = C - P, (D mod 2) = 0, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, D = C - P, (D mod 2) = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- not row_has_one(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% No colour outside the allowed set
:- output(R, C, Color), not allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

% Identify rows with exactly one non‑zero cell
row_has_one(R) :- #count {C : input(R, C, V), V != 0} = 1.

% The unique non‑zero cell in such a row
pivot(R, P, V) :- row_has_one(R), input(R, P, V), V != 0.

% Every input cell
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Possible colours for each cell
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, D = C - P, (D mod 2) = 0, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, D = C - P, (D mod 2) = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- not row_has_one(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% Disallow any colour that is not allowed
:- output(R, C, Color), not allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>
```

row_has_one(R) :- #count {C : input(R, C, V), V != 0} = 1.

% forbid any colour that is not allowed
:- output(R, C, Color), not allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

```
<asp>
% Identify rows that contain exactly one non‑zero cell
row_has_one(R) :- #count {C : input(R, C, V), V != 0} = 1.

% The unique non‑zero cell in such a row
pivot(R, P, V) :- row_has_one(R), input(R, P, V), V != 0.

% Every cell of the input grid (all positions are present)
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, (C - P) mod 2 = 0, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, (C - P) mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- not row_has_one(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% Disallow any colour that is not allowed
:- output(R, C, Color), not allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% count distinct rows and columns of the input
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% a cell in the output grid must receive a colour
cell(R,C) :- R < N, C < M.

% start cells are the non‑zero cells of the input
start(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% there is a start in this row whose column is not to the right of C
cover(R,C) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0.

% parity even: (C‑C0) is a multiple of 2
even(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F.

% parity odd: (C‑C0) is one more than a multiple of 2
odd(R,C,V) :- start(R,C0,V), C >= C0, (C - C0) # = 2*F + 1.

% allowed colour for a cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- even(R,C,V), Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- odd(R,C,V),   Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not cover(R,C), Color = 0.

% exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed
:- cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).

% count the number of rows and columns of the input
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% cell (row, col) is inside the input grid
cell(R,C) :- R >= 0, C >= 0, R < NR, C < NC.

% count non‑zero cells in each row
nz(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% pivot: the (only) non‑zero cell of a row with exactly one such cell
pivot(R,P,V) :- input(R,P,V), V != 0, nz(R,Nz), Nz = 1.

% ----- allowed colours for every cell -----
% rows without any non‑zero cell: all cells must be 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- nz(R,Nz), Nz = 0, cell(R,C).

% the pivot cell keeps the original colour
allowed(R,P,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), cell(R,P).

% cells left of the pivot are forced to 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with even offset keep the original colour
allowed(R,C,V) :- pivot(R,P,V), even_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% cells to the right of the pivot with odd offset become colour 5
allowed(R,C,5) :- pivot(R,P,V), odd_offset(R,C,P,F), cell(R,C).

% parity helpers (used by the two rules above)
even_offset(R,C,P,F) :- C > P, C - P = 2*F.
odd_offset(R,C,P,F)  :- C > P, C - P = 2*F + 1.

% exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% forbid any colour that is not allowed for the cell
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

<asp>
% Non‑zero cells
nonzero(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Row has exactly one non‑zero cell
row_one(R,P,V) :- nonzero(R,P,V), #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 1.

% Row has no non‑zero cells
row_none(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} = 0.

% Row has more than one non‑zero cell
row_multi(R) :- #count{C : nonzero(R,C,W)} > 1.

% Row and column predicates (all rows and columns that appear in the input)
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

% Every cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_one(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) # mod 2 = 0, Color = V.

% rows without any non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), Color = 0.

% rows with more than one non‑zero cell: colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Disallow any colour that is not permitted
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
</asp>

% Rows that have exactly one non‑zero cell
row_has_one(R) :- #count { C : input(R, C, W), W != 0 } = 1.

% Rows with no non‑zero cells (also treats rows with >1 non‑zero as “zero rows”)
row_zero(R) :- #count { C : input(R, C, W), W != 0 } = 0.

% The unique non‑zero cell in a row that has exactly one such cell
pivot(R, P, V) :- row_has_one(R), input(R, P, V), V != 0.

% Every cell of the input grid (all positions are present)
cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% Possible colours for each cell
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, (C - P) mod 2 = 0, Color = V.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- pivot(R, P, V), C > P, (C - P) mod 2 = 1, Color = 5.
allowed(R, C, Color) :- row_zero(R), Color = 0.

% Exactly one colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C).

% No colour outside the allowed set
:- output(R, C, Color), not allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

Thus final program:

```
% define rows and columns
row(R) :- input(R,_,_).
col(C) :- input(_,C,_).

cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% count non-zero per row
row_nz_cnt(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

row_one(R) :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 1.
row_none(R) :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 0.
row_multi(R) :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz > 1.

% pivot for row_one
pivot(R,P,V) :- row_one(R), input(R,P,V), V != 0.

% allowed colors
% rows with exactly one non-zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) = 2*F, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) = 2*F + 1, Color = 5.

% rows with no non-zero cells
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_none(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% rows with multiple non-zero cells -> all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% disallow colors not allowed
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
```

```
% Count non‑zero cells per row
row_nz_cnt(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

row_has_one(R) :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 1.
row_zero(R)   :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 0.
row_multi(R)  :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz > 1.

% Unique non‑zero in rows with exactly one non‑zero
pivot(R,P,V) :- row_has_one(R), input(R,P,V), V != 0.

% each cell of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% allowed colours
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C = P, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C < P, Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) = 2*F, Color = V.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C > P, (C - P) = 2*F + 1, Color = 5.

% rows without non‑zero cells become all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_zero(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% rows with more than one non‑zero cell become all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% exactly one output colour for every cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% disallow colours not permitted
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
```

refinements.response:
```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Row‑wise rule inferred from the three training examples
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% Count how many non‑zero cells a row contains
row_nz_cnt(R,Nz) :- Nz = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }.

% Row categories
row_has_one(R) :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 1.
row_zero(R)   :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz = 0.
row_multi(R)  :- row_nz_cnt(R,Nz), Nz > 1.

% The unique non‑zero cell in a “one‑non‑zero” row
pivot(R,P,V) :- row_has_one(R), input(R,P,V), V != 0.

% Every position that occurs in the input (including zeros)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Allowed colours for each cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Row with exactly one non‑zero cell
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C = P,      Color = V.   % pivot column
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C <  P,     Color = 0.   % left of pivot
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C >  P, (C - P) = 2*F,     Color = V. % right, even offset
allowed(R,C,Color) :- pivot(R,P,V), C >  P, (C - P) = 2*F + 1, Color = 5. % right, odd offset

% Rows without any non‑zero cell become all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_zero(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% Rows with more than one non‑zero cell become all zero
allowed(R,C,Color) :- row_multi(R), cell(R,C), Color = 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% No colour may be chosen outside the allowed set
:- output(R,C,Color), not allowed(R,C,Color).
```
